title: The Bells Of Heaven
content:
'Twould ring the bells of Heaven
    The wildest peal for years,
    If Parson lost his senses
    And people came to theirs,
    And he and they together
    Knelt down with angry prayers
    For tamed and shabby tigers
    And dancing dogs and bears,
    And wretched, blind pit ponies,
    And little hunted hares.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Ralph Hodgson delivers a powerful performance in "The Bells Of Heaven"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
